name: l7_hole_emergency_gate_80
description: Enter survival mode as soon as hole count exceeds 80 to prevent collapse trajectories on level-7-like boards.
llm_notes: Failure episodes average 99.66 holes vs 75.22 in successes (delta 24.44) while max height differs by only 0.41 (20.00 vs 19.59), so hole-threshold gating should separate terminal states earlier than height-only control.

name: l7_opening_hole_penalty_0_24_v3
description: Apply stronger hole suppression for pieces 0-24 to stabilize the opening-to-midgame transition.
llm_notes: Failures end after 23.23 pieces on average with 99.66 holes, and outlier episodes show lower early holes (78.8) than baseline early holes (85.7), indicating the fragile window extends beyond the first 12 pieces.

name: l7_surface_volatility_brake_height_gate_90
description: Keep bumpiness suppression global but intensify survival behavior once stack height exceeds 90% of board capacity.
llm_notes: Bumpiness is the largest separator (7.92 failure vs 1.44 success, delta 6.48), and level 7 average height is already 19.63, so adding a high-height gate tests whether anti-volatility pressure should spike only in terminal-risk states.
